subject: [RFC PATCH] mm: bypass swap readahead for zswap

reviews.author: Kairui Song
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that bypassing swap readahead for zswap is an interesting idea but did not provide any specific technical feedback or concerns.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Alexandre

Thanks for the test and patch, very interesting idea.

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: The reviewer suggested moving the bypass logic for zswap readahead into a separate, named function within the swap code to improve abstraction and maintainability.
reviews.raw_body:
This should really be abstracted into a reasonably-named helper that can live in
swap code.

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Nhat Pham
reviews.summary: Reviewer Nhat Pham agreed with the patch, suggesting an additional improvement by calling xa_empty() to further optimize zswap performance.
reviews.raw_body:
+1.

Maybe also xa_empty() when we're at it? :)

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: The reviewer questioned the assumption that if a swap entry is in zswap, then all subsequent readahead entries will also be in zswap, pointing out that it's possible for remaining entries to be on disk instead.
reviews.raw_body:
This assumes that if the swap entry is in zswap, then the remaining
entries (covered by the readahead window) will also be in zswap,
right? While not very likely, it's possible that the remaining entries
not in zswap but on disk, right?

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch bypasses swap readahead for zswap, an in-memory compression backend similar to zram. The current implementation of swap readahead is optimized for traditional disk-based swap devices and can cause issues like inflated anon residency, page cache thrashing, and increased fault latency when applied to an in-memory backend. By skipping readahead for zswap, the patch aims to improve performance by reducing unnecessary prefetching of pages that are not needed. The results from testing this bypass show significant improvements in build time, swap-in/out rates, and reduced swap readahead hits.

reviews.author: Barry Song
reviews.summary: Reviewer Barry Song noted that readahead swap entries in zswap can lead to unnecessary decompression and additional page faults, and suggested a proof-of-concept patch to enable fault-around-like behavior for do_swap_page() when the readahead folios are in zswap.

2026-06-25.patch_summary: This patch bypasses swap readahead for zswap, an in-memory compression backend similar to zram. The current implementation of swap readahead is optimized for traditional disk-based swap devices and can cause issues like inflated anon residency, page cache thrashing, and increased fault latency when applied to an in-memory backend. By skipping readahead for zswap, the patch aims to improve performance by reducing unnecessary prefetching of pages that are not needed. The results from testing this bypass show significant improvements in build time, swap-in/out rates, and reduced swap readahead hits.
